structured_payload_candidate.title: Endpoints as a Function of Zone Group Mode
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit − Variable-Volume Fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Defines the active cooling maximum and minimum airflow endpoints as a function of Zone Group mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Cooling maximum endpoint is Vcool-max in Occupied, Cooldown, Setup; 0 in Warmup, Setback, Unoccupied. Minimum endpoint is Vmin in Occupied; 0 in all other modes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Endpoint  Occupied  Cooldown  Setup  Warmup  Setback  Unoccupied
Cooling maximum  Vcool -max Vcool -max Vcool -max 0 0 0
Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling State Control Sequence
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit − Variable-Volume Fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When Zone State is Cooling, map cooling loop output to primary airflow setpoint from min to cooling max endpoint, limit primary and fan airflow to minimums if AHU SAT > room temperature, map cooling loop output to series fan airflow from max(OA-min, primary min) to cooling max endpoint, and keep heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When the Zone State Is Cooling
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Primary airflow setpoint mapped from cooling loop output from minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint. If AHU SAT > room temperature, primary airflow setpoint limited to minimum endpoint and series fan airflow setpoint limited to OA-min. Series fan airflow setpoint mapped from cooling loop output from larger of OA-min and primary airflow minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. The cooling -loop output shall be mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint .
1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active primary airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint and the series fan airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than OA -min.
b. The Cooling Loop  output shall be mapped to the series fan airflow setpoint  from the larger of OA-min and the primary airflow minimum endpoint  to the cooling maximum endpoint .
c. Heating coil is OFF.

b. The Cooling Loop  output shall be mapped to the series fan airflow setpoint  from the larger of OA-min and the primary airflow minimum endpoint  to the cooling maximum endpoint .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband State Control Sequence
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit − Variable-Volume Fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When Zone State is Deadband, set primary airflow setpoint to minimum endpoint, series fan airflow setpoint to OA-min, and heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: When the Zone State Is Deadband
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Primary airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint. Series fan airflow setpoint = OA-min. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. The primary airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint .
b. The series fan airflow setpoint  shall be equal to OA -min.
c. Heating coil is OFF.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating State Control Sequence
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit − Variable-Volume Fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When Zone State is Heating, use heating loop output 0-50% to reset discharge temperature setpoint from AHU SAT to MaxΔT above space setpoint with primary airflow at minimum and fan at OA-min; from 50-100% reset series fan airflow from OA-min to Sfan-htgmax while primary remains at minimum; modulate heating coil to maintain discharge temperature setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: From 0% to 50% heating loop: reset DAT setpoint from current AHU SAT to MaxΔT above space setpoint, primary airflow at minimum endpoint, fan airflow at OA-min. From 50% to 100%: reset fan airflow from OA-min to Sfan-htgmax, primary airflow at minimum endpoint. Heating coil modulated to maintain DAT at setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. From 0 % to 50%, the Heating Loop  output shall reset the discharge temperature setpoint  from the current AHU SAT setpoint  to a maximum of Max∆T above space temperature setpoint . The primary airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint , and the series fan airflow setpoint shall be OA -min.
b. From 50 % to 100%, the Heating Loop  output shall reset the series fan airflow setpoint  from OA-min to a Sfan -htgmax. The active primary airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint .
c. The heating coil shall be modulated to maintain th e discharge temperature at setpoint . (Directly controlling heating off zone temperature control loop is not acceptable).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Series Fan Control Logic
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit − Variable-Volume Fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan runs whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting fan, drive damper fully closed. After fan proven ON for 15 seconds, release damper override.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is heating or cooling, or Zone Group Occupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fan shall run. Before start, damper fully closed. Once fan proven ON for 15 s, damper override released.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating  or cooling Zone State , or if the associated Zone Group  is in Occupied Mode . Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to ensure that the fan is not rotating backward.  Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), t he damper override is released.

evidence_quote: Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating  or cooling Zone State , or if the associated Zone Group  is in Occupied Mode . Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to ensure that the fan is not rotating backward.  Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), t he damper override is released.
evidence_text: Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating  or cooling Zone State , or if the associated Zone Group  is in Occupied Mode . Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to ensure that the fan is not rotating backward.  Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), t he damper override is released.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Logic Design Guidance
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit − Variable-Volume Fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The heating logic leads with supply air temperature increase before fan speed increase, assuming the plenum air temperature may be below space temperature. If designer is confident the plenum is always warmer, the logic can be reversed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: In the heating Zone State , the logic keeps the fan airflow rate low while supply air temperature is increased as the first heating stage. This presumes that the temperature of the air the fan is supplying is neutral or below the space temperature, as it wo uld be if the fan draws air directly from the space , and as it might be if the fan draws air from a return air plenum that is cooled by roof and wall heat losses. In the past, return air plenums were warmed by recessed light fixtures, but pendent  lights ar e increasingly  common , so the potential for free heating from the plenum is smaller than it was. Because  there is the potential that the plenum is colder than the space due to envelope loads, the logic leads with the supply air temperature rather than with an increase in fan speed. If the designer is confident that the plenum will always be warmer, the logic  can be reversed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling SAT Reset Request Generation
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit − Variable-Volume Fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate cooling supply air temperature reset requests based on zone temperature deviation from cooling setpoint and cooling loop demand.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone temperature exceeding cooling setpoint or cooling loop output high
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: If zone temp > cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 min after suppression, send 3 requests. Else if > by 2°C (3°F) for 2 min after suppression, send 2 requests. Else if Cooling Loop > 95%, send 1 request until < 85%. Else if Cooling Loop < 95%, send 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 3 requests.
b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.
c. Else if the Cooling Loop  is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the Cooling Loop  is less than 85%.
d. Else if the Cooling Loop  is les s than 95%, send 0 requests.
